opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: They cancelled my surgery after two years of waiting. I can't do this anymore — the system wants me dead. My heart is pounding, I can't stop shaking, I can't catch my breath, and I haven't slept in two days.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Since the phone call yesterday saying the surgery is off, I've had a panic I can't switch off — racing heart, breathless, and no sleep at all. My partner made me call.

symptom_narrative.onset: yesterday, straight after a phone call telling me the surgery was cancelled
symptom_narrative.duration: about a day and a half, non-stop
symptom_narrative.character: heart pounding, shaking all over, can't catch my breath, waves of panic and despair
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst I have ever felt — like I can't keep going
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: my chest and my whole body
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since the call, worse whenever I look at the paperwork
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: the insurance denial letters and appeal forms, thinking about the fight ahead of me
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no sleep at all for two days, exhausted but can't rest, pacing, can't sit still
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've had long-running anxiety about all of this but never a crash like this one
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't work, can't rest, can't function at all today

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: I'm a transgender woman and I've been on the waiting list for gender-affirming surgery
past_medical_history.how_managed: under a gender-affirming care pathway and prescribed hormone therapy

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: Transgender; two years on a surgery waiting list, just told it's indefinitely delayed. Constantly fighting insurance denials and appeals, with big out-of-pocket costs. Frightened by the political climate around trans healthcare and worried about losing access to my hormones, so I've been skipping doses to stockpile them. My partner is here with me.

communication_style.typical_message_style: long, emotionally intense messages; keeps returning to the sense of being failed and dehumanised by the system

access_barriers.financial: large out-of-pocket costs and repeated insurance denials for affirming care
access_barriers.other: administrative gatekeeping; fear of losing access to hormone medication

objective_data_offered.value: visibly trembling and shaking, tearful, pacing and unable to sit still, speaking rapidly
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: observed over video; not a clinician examination

objective_data_offered.value: partner reports she has not slept at all for about 48 hours and has been skipping doses of her hormone medication to stockpile it
my heart feels like it is pounding and I can't slow my breathing down
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient-reported sensation, no device reading provided
